mentors.name: Yannik Sauerborn
mentors.application_message:
Hi, my name is Yannik. I’m a Psychologist based in Melbourne. I came across your program through a social media ad and was really impressed by the work you’re doing. I’m interested to know whether your team includes psychologists or other clinicians, and whether you’re open to potential collaboration.

I work in a clinic specialising in boys and men mental health, and I haven’t seen a program quite like yours before. I’m considering it as a referral option, but I’m also curious about ways I might be able to contribute or support the work you’re doing. Let me know. Cheers Yannik

mentors.name: Liam Stacey
mentors.application_message: Harry Lockwood told me about the work you guys do and it sounds like really meaningful work. Currently I am studying psychology and have completed my first 2 years of undergrad. Through this I am doing a minor in mental health and have become mental health first aid accredited. Out of uni I am a cub leader where I lead kids aged 8-11 in a variety of activities, weekend camps and help them through issues, build confidence, develop leadership skills and get outside in nature. I’m loving studying psychology as it’s an area that I’m very passionate in and I believe this will only help me develop my skills, I’m also loving being a cub leader as it’s great to see when the kids come out of their shell and have a bunch of fun doing activities they were originally scared to try and from the sounds of it this would be very similar.

mentors.name: Dylan
mentors.application_message: I’m Dylan, 28 years old and have had my fair share of problems growing up which I love now to pass on support to young kids and men. I’m currently a support worker for my own disability support work business and also have been a personal trainer for the last 3 years. I’d love to chat further.

mentors.name: Josh
mentors.application_message: 20 year old male who is working through his 20s aspirations to be a fire fighter one day and have seen what support groups and mentors can do for many types of people and look to help the male youth community in some way shape or form as many can see it’s not the same as it used to be.

mentors.name: Angus
mentors.application_message: Third year student at The ANU, lots of experience in leadership roles and working with younger people (at school, uni, and work). Background in eating disorder prevention and support.

mentors.name: Will Martin
mentors.application_message: Hey guys, My names Will, I’m 21 and am good mates with a few of the boys doing mentoring, I catch myself forward planning a little too much sometimes and things obviously don’t always fall into line the way you want them to and I think the way I’ve dealt with it all is something to be proud of and something I can share with a few of kids looking for help here. Cheers Will

mentors.name: Lucy Dixon
mentors.application_message:
28years old 
Love mentoring, I have worked in aviation for nearly 10 years, I have also been a trainer for a lot of that time.
I have 2 kids and love spending time outsides. 
I’m looking for some additional work and to also make a difference in someone day

mentors.name: Jack Luscombe
mentors.application_message: I'm 21 years old and love all things sport. I currently play footy and cricket and have also coached both over the past 3 years, currently working with year 9s. I'd love the opportunity to work as a mentor to more teenagers to help try and make a difference in their lives if I can.

mentors.name: Oskar
mentors.application_message:
My name is Oskar Smartt
I’m in a family of 6 my parents and 3 siblings,  and a dog
I played a lot of sports growing up a kid 
I played 5 AFL games for Essendon 
And I would love to help mentor kids into becoming better in life

mentors.name: Josh Fedele
mentors.application_message: Hey my name is Josh. I am currently studying a Bachelor of Mathematics and Computer Sciences at university.

mentors.name: Charlie Meadows
mentors.application_message:
I am a driven and approachable individual with a strong passion for sport, leadership, and supporting young men. I attended St Joseph’s College (Joeys), where I was named School Captain and Football Captain, experiences that shaped my confidence, empathy, and ability to lead with integrity. I am known for being charismatic, energetic, and comforting, with a natural ability to connect with others and make them feel understood.
Alongside studying at RMIT University, I manage three part-time jobs and currently provide support to Year 7 students at St Joseph’s College, helping them navigate personal and social challenges. I am deeply committed to mentoring young men and supporting their growth, resilience, and wellbeing.

mentors.name: Cooper
mentors.application_message: At 19, I would love to have a positive influence on th younger generation in regards to mental health. We all battle our own mind and I would love to mentor people into the right headspace. I have coached younger kids in basketball and understand what they need to succeed and how to communicate with them. I would appreciate an opportunity to help them develop and become better people

mentors.name: Tom
mentors.application_message: I’m a 21 year old from Adelaide, currently in my final year of a Commerce degree at the University of Adelaide. I have a strong passion for sport, especially playing and watching basketball and AFL. I’m highly social and enjoy connecting with people, and I’m particularly interested in opportunities that allow me to support and positively impact kids, as helping others is something I genuinely enjoy.

mentors.name: Zachary Fryer
mentors.application_message: I’m Zac, 26 and come from a fitness and personal training background. A big part of what I enjoy is helping young guys build solid habits, confidence, and structure that carry over into everyday life, not just the gym. I’ve always connected well with younger guys because I remember being in that stage myself, which makes it easy to relate and have honest conversations. I believe being able to help shape young men into strong, grounded leaders in the community is a passion I’ve developed through my own experiences.

mentors.name: Genna
mentors.application_message: I’m currently working as an Aged Care worker I have studied Allied health which opens the pathway for OT although I specialise in physical education my specialty is rehab

mentors.name: Dylan
mentors.application_message: I'm a 20 year old going into my third year of studying a Bachelor of Science Advanced Global Challenges at Monash University. Aside from studying, I work as a Youth Minister at St Bedes College Mentone. Within this role I facilitate social justice events, student retreat days and our youth leaders group. I am passionate about men's mental health, respect and individuality. Having been College Captain of St Bede's College in 2023, I understand the challenges of all boys education, which have helped me to understand the nuances of the challenges facing young men today. I'm really interested to learn more about this organisation and to explore a potential fit.

mentors.name: Mia Doherty
mentors.application_message:
Growing up, I faced a number of challenges that shaped who I am today. As a child, I often wished I had a mentor—someone consistent and supportive who could guide me, listen to me, and help me believe in my potential. Not having that support has strongly influenced my desire to become a mentor myself. I want to be the person I once needed and provide young people with encouragement, understanding, and a safe space to grow.

When I was younger, I played netball, which taught me the importance of teamwork, resilience, and building positive relationships. I’ve always enjoyed connecting with others and spending time with my friends and family, and I value strong, supportive communities. I am currently studying a Bachelor of Criminology and plan to commence a Master of Social Work next year, as I am deeply passionate about working with and advocating for young people. Becoming a mentor would allow me to combine my lived experience, education, and genuine care for others to make a meaningful difference in a young person’s life.

mentors.name: Darcy
mentors.application_message: I’m turning 19 in March and really striving to have the positive impact on young people the same way I’ve been privileged to receive. Leadership has always been important to me and I’ve grown my understanding of it over the years significantly. This has come through multiple forms such as being the school captain of Geelong high in 2024 and volunteering for multiple organisations that serve a very similar purpose as this. These include the lifechanger mentor program in year 10 and being a Read the Play youth ambassador creating a mental health driven program to sporting clubs in my region. One that I hold close to my heart is my ongoing work with YLead where I attended as a participant and have since gone on to be a mentor, a guru and help out at in school programs over the last couple of years. It’s here that I really grew my passion for helping young people be the best versions of themselves especially young males. As a man of faith, guiding others away from the dark paths of the modern world is very important to me and I have proven to be highly capable at this. I love my sport, being active, spending time with friends and family, meaningful connection and really making a difference. Currently still working at Woolworths and in Uni, but I’m on the job hunt but don’t just want to work in a new store/restaurant/cafe, I wish to utilise my skills and make a really positive impact on others. Sounds like this is the perfect gig for where I’m at right now, appreciate any consideration, programs doing and will do great things regardless so all the best no matter the decision!!!

mentors.name: Chayse O’Shea
mentors.application_message: As an 18 year old who graduated high school last year I know how important it is to have a mentor and role model that you can rely on throughout teenagehood. I’ve always thought that teenagers mental health issues were overlooked and not seen as “real” issues as if adults can only be the ones who suffer. I think this company contradicts all those beliefs and treats the issue with the seriousness, empathy, and respect it deserves, recognising that young people’s struggles are just as valid and impactful. As someone who has struggled with various challenges throughout high school, I want to be able to give back and be apart of something that truly matters. Sometimes it’s not just about getting rid of the problem but about having something good you can lean on when the problems are there.

mentors.name: Ethan Holm
mentors.application_message:
I’m someone who naturally connects by getting alongside people rather than trying to lead from the front. I’m not loud or “in your face” — I’d describe myself more as steady and approachable. I listen first, take people seriously, and try to create the kind of space where someone feels comfortable enough to open up at their own pace. I think trust grows quietly, through consistency more than big moments.

Relationships are a big part of who I am. I really value my friendships, and some of my favourite moments are the simple ones — sharing music (my taste is pretty eclectic), having long conversations, or sitting around the family dinner table where discussions can move from deep to light-hearted without warning. Those experiences have shaped how I relate to people: connection comes before advice, and feeling understood matters more than having the perfect words.

My Christian faith is also an important foundation in my life. It shapes how I try to treat people — with patience, respect, and a genuine sense of care. I believe everyone benefits from having someone who shows up consistently, keeps their word, and chooses encouragement over judgment.

At this stage of life, I find myself thinking more about purpose and impact — not just what I’m doing, but the kind of person I’m becoming. I don’t believe young guys need someone who has everything figured out. They need someone real. Someone dependable. Someone willing to sit in the conversation, celebrate progress, and remind them they don’t have to work things out alone.

If I had to sum myself up, I’d say I value meaningful conversations, quiet consistency, and being the kind of person who makes others feel seen. Sometimes the strongest influence isn’t pushing someone forward — it’s walking beside them while they figure things out themselves.

mentors.name: Jason
mentors.application_message:
Hi There
I am a 50 year old father and would like to see how I may be able to help out. I saw your add for Mentors on seek and realise my age doesn’t fit with the add. How else could I provide some help?

mentors.name: Jack Stephenson
mentors.application_message:
Hi, 
I'm Jack from the UK and just moved to Australia on a WHV. Hopefully for at least 3 years! I've struggled with anxiety most of my life, on and off, but gradually have learnt the best ways to deal with it. I'm 25 years of age, constantly strive to be a better individual but I've been stuck in kitchen work for the last 5 years which isn't giving me the personal challenges and fulfilment that I desire. I have experience as a mentor, working in my spare time coaching kids how to play soccer and I love that feeling of making a difference. I saw this opportunity and immediately thought it would suit me, would love to hear back from you. 
Thanks so much Jack!

mentors.name: Anisa Darcy
mentors.application_message:
I’m someone who has reached a point in my career where I’m actively seeking work that feels more purpose-driven and genuinely impactful. I’ve spent several years in performance-led marketing and operations roles, where I’ve learned how to run systems, manage people, and deliver outcomes, but increasingly, I’m motivated by the idea of contributing to something that changes lives, not just metrics.

While I’m not a male myself, I’m deeply aligned with the mission behind Blues & Greens. I’ve seen first-hand how powerful consistent support, accountability, and genuine connection can be in a young person’s life - and how damaging it can be when that support is missing. The idea of helping create environments where teenagers feel seen, backed, and guided resonates strongly with me.

What draws me to Blues & Greens specifically is the focus on character, consistency, and showing up; values that I try to live by in my own life. Whether through mentoring directly or contributing through operational, community, or support roles, I want to be part of a team that’s committed to doing meaningful work with care and integrity.

At this stage, I’m open-minded about how I can contribute best. What matters most to me is being involved in work that has real human impact and supporting a mission that’s bigger than any one individual.

Thank you for your time.

mentors.name: Kovacs Josh
mentors.application_message:
10 years in the fitness helping people achieve their goals physically and mentally of all fitness levels and goals from beginner to elite athletes. 
I’ve authored and published my book “essentially you”a personal development book for teens creating thought and mindfulness to achieve there’s most out of yourself for yourself.
 
I’m also a father of two and I was a full time single parent with my son for 9 years , with my guidance and support my son graduated high school and has completed his apprenticeship as a plumber. 
With my vast life experience and natural ability to connect easily with others I’m able to support and help to suit. 
I’m also a mental health first aider

mentors.name: Samuel Waters
mentors.application_message:
Hi! I’m Sam, I’m a university student from North Brisbane, and I study psychology. I’m also a tutor, and I really enjoy working with kids and teens and helping them build confidence in themselves and their learning. A big reason I’m drawn to youth mentoring is that my own teen years weren’t great, and that’s part of why I chose to study psychology — I want to be the kind of supportive adult that I wish I’d had at that age.

I’ve worked in people-focused roles (including youth-facing customer service), which has helped me get good at reading the room, building rapport quickly, and staying calm and supportive when someone’s feeling anxious, frustrated, or disengaged. I care a lot about creating safe, non-judgmental spaces where young people feel comfortable being themselves and actually feel listened to. I’m easy to talk to, patient, and genuinely motivated to help teens feel more supported, understood, and confident in who they are.

mentors.name: Samuel Riddlestone-Holmes
mentors.application_message:
Hello, my name is Sam. I came across the position as a teen mentor for Blues and Greens, and what you're doing really interests me as someone who had a rough time growing up and managed to come through the other side stronger.

I feel like I’ve experienced almost all the trials that can be thrown at someone during youth. I had an abusive father and a drug-dependant mother who divorced when I was quite young. I suffered from severe depression and anxiety from a young age and was highly medicated from the age of 10, spending periods of time in mental health facilities for my own safety. I was severely bullied throughout my school life and was known as “the mute,” as I did not speak to anybody or try to defend myself.

My mother eventually kicked me out of the house when I was 14 years old. I spent a year couch surfing before eventually being placed in a refuge, where I spent the next few years living in a very chaotic household among other troubled youth. My involvement with some of these individuals unfortunately led me down a regrettable path of criminal activity, for which I have already repaid my debt to society. By the time I reached adulthood, I was a high school dropout. I couldn’t hold down a job for more than a week. I cycled through countless therapists and medications.

However, over the years, through my own determination for a better life and with the support of some very good people around me, I managed to turn my life around and have since become a very capable and competent person. I completed my high school equivalent at TAFE and then went on to university, where I earned a bachelor’s degree. After that, I started my own freelance business creating 3D animated music videos. For the past few years, I have been living out my dream of traveling the world, something I never thought would be possible when I was younger, when I couldn’t see a future beyond the next day.

I was able to turn my life around, but it’s hard to say whether that would have been possible if I hadn’t been fortunate enough to have a few people offer me support when I needed it most. I would love to use my knowledge and experience to help other young men who feel lost on their path and might need a helping hand or some guidance.

I understand the position is aimed at 18 to 25 year olds. I am 30, and I completely understand if that does not align with the requirements, but I wanted to offer my help in case it could still be of value. I have never done something like this before, but I believe I may be able to offer hope to young men who are in need of it.

mentors.name: Bel
mentors.application_message:
Hello, The program looks great. Id like to have a conversation with whoever is managing the hiring of mentors. Id like to know some details about the work and the containment of the job hours. Id also like to know about risk management and psychosocial safety for employees. 


Thanks in advance. 
Warmly,
Bel

mentors.name: Lockie Ranson
mentors.application_message:
Hi there,

I'm Lockie. I'm currently living on the central coast of NSW, always looking for deeper connection and healthy community. Since I was in school, I have felt the universal need for mentorship and positive community for young men and boys. Going to an all boys school in Perth I saw the yearning for acceptance and the repercussions when boys felt isolated and alone. In my final year at school I was appointed School Captain by peer vote - I believe this was because of my ability to connect with the range of personalities and groups across my cohort. 

Although I do not have have lived experience with addiction, 18 months ago I was injured in a car accident and lost two fingers on my right hand. This was a wake up call for me - the preciousness of life and the cruelty at how easy it is to move on to the next. Part of my realisations were to stop ignoring the obvious impulses and signs. Since then, two friends and I have been creating spaces and retreats for men to run wild like the young boys inside and then also gather as authentic expressive men, making room for conversations, questions, ideas to be explored. 

Outside of this, I enjoy weights, running, surfing (and other ocean sports), yoga, music festivals, wood work and playing the guitar. As of the next two months I'm in Indonesia training to be a breathwork facilitator - I believe this mentorship program, the mens retreats and breathwork are all powerfully aligned to make a difference for young men and their communities. I believe that we all have light within us. As we go through life, there are challenges, traumas and sacrifices we make that pull curtains across this light. I also believe it is part of my purpose to help people draw back these curtains so that their light can shine warm, bright, authentic and expressive. 

Thank you so much for taking time out of your day to read a bit about me - I'm sure you might have questions and want to know more about me and I look forward to having those conversations with you. Please don't hesitate to reach out. 

Chat soon,
Lockie

mentors.name: Qwaedym Moka Colin Gerald Awhitu
mentors.application_message: Hey my name is qwaedym, I know crazy name. Anyways I’m a 24 year old father that just love people, I love engaging in activities with stranger or speaking to people I don’t know or going up to people and blessing their days with a smile on my face. I’m one of the extreme cases of extrovert. I’ve worked many many jobs just purely because I feel like I don’t belong or I feel like what I do at those jobs weren’t achieving anything. But what this company stands for is exactly what I would love to do and would love to have the opportunity to not only better my life but to also better others. I’m also an older brother to 6 siblings and there’s nothing more that I want then to see them achieve individual goals that they want to accomplish. So with that said I believe this company and everything it stands for is what I was brought onto this earth to do. Would love to hear back. Thank you.

mentors.name: Gary Chen
mentors.application_message:
Hi. I am Gary, 18 years old, 18-year-old high shool graduate from the class of 2025. I first found out about Blues & Greens via Harry Q’s seek.com job offer for “self-improvement junkies” aged 18-25 and it intrigued me. Although I do have to be honest that the reason I was on the website in the first place was that I was looking for a new job to earn some income, this job’s description actually matched a lot of terms on my current life. 1) I do regular exercise (basketball) which proves that I do have basic discipline when it comes to fitness & physical health/wellbeing, 2) I have found myself growing further apart from my old friend group and this would be the perfect opportunity for me to meet a new friend that I can talk to on a weekly basis (on top of helping them with their problems which will benefit me in the future especially if I decide to pursue a path in hospitality), 3) I grew up as a very introverted kid all the way up to years 5/6 and I understand how it feels being alone, feeling that no one sees you while you so desperately want to make a change but cannot, along with pressure from both myself and parents to change. I wish to also help teenagers in similar circumstances right now to avoid or at least ease the depression that it causes since I had no one to talk about it with, I want to be that person for them. I wish to also learn as well as be a mentor to make myself a better human. 

Interests: video games, sports and cars. 

Please contact me if the job application offer is still available.

mentors.name: Caleb Michael Pearce
mentors.brodie_notes: VM, sounds like a very non enthusiatic person from ringtone

mentors.name: Ethan Neill
mentors.application_message:
Hey team! I am Ethan from Dubbo but living and loving life in Bundaberg and surrounds work with cattle. I used to volunteer with headspace and youthinsearch but have been living life by myself for a while, and excitedly looking for my next adventure. Love ya's
Looking forward to hearing from you

mentors.name: KRISTIAN
mentors.application_message: I am an avid reader and group fitness lover who is passionate about exercise in movement. I also enjoy spending time outdoors , cooking and exploring bookstores and libraries

mentors.name: Bailey George ison
mentors.application_message: I’m a 21 yo looking to help get teens onto the right path and help anyway they need
mentors.brodie_notes: Doesnt have a laptop and only 4-6 hours. Dont think its worth the hassle

mentors.name: Jared Crosbie
mentors.application_message: Hi my names Jared. I currently work as an Enrolled Nurse in General medicine specialising in all ages. I’m currently studying my bachelor of nursing at University. I’m interested in making a difference in people’s lives and what you do interests me a lot! Look forward to hearing from you!

mentors.name: Dheer Dojin Shah
mentors.brodie_notes: Hadnt had time to do it? Doing it tonight

mentors.name: Jayden Eisel
mentors.brodie_notes: Test gorilla got sent to spam… doing it now

mentors.name: Ryan Hart
mentors.application_message:
Hi, I'm Ryan - a 27 year old medical student from Fremantle, dedicated to making a difference. I have 5 years’ experience in healthcare and 3 years’ experience as a Marketing Officer for a med tech company. Mentoring has always been a part of my life as I've had roles as a tennis coach and piano teacher for adolscent's in primary school. I felt a great sense of purpose from building relationships and acting as a positive role model for my pupils.

I have just come off the back of my paediatrics rotation at med school and realised how much I had missed working with kids and the satisfaction I felt from doing good. My friend Benjamin Philip, referred me to this role and explained how rewarding is has been for him this year. I have a great respect for the mission statement of Blues & Greens and believe that I can contribute to better the lives of the youth through your organisation.

mentors.name: Isaiah Lee
mentors.application_message:
I am 21 year old Uni student studying to become a Veterinarian. 

I played competitive Table Tennis up until year 12 and have a passion for human connection and believe it is one of the most valuable things in life. 

I enjoy the beach, golf, running and DJing in my time off.

mentors.name: howard johnson
mentors.application_message:
I am writing to express my strong interest in the mentor role currently available. I have been following Blues and Greens for the past few months and have loved watching it go from strength to strength. Seeing Harry’s journey has been incredibly motivating; it is humbling to watch someone find something they are truly passionate about at such a young age and take a leap of faith to create meaningful change in the world.
This strongly resonates with me. Last year, I made the decision to move to Perth to pursue a career in education, and I can honestly say it has been the best decision I’ve ever made. Taking that step reinforced my belief in the power of purpose-driven work and the importance of having the right guidance and support along the way.
I am 31 years old and currently working in outdoor education, where I support young people in developing confidence, resilience, teamwork, and practical life skills. Through this role, I have gained extensive experience mentoring individuals from diverse backgrounds, building positive relationships, and providing guidance in both structured and challenging environments. These experiences have strengthened my ability to listen, communicate clearly, and support others in achieving personal and developmental goals.
While my professional background is rooted in outdoor education, the core skills I use daily—mentorship, leadership, empathy, and adaptability—align closely with the responsibilities of a mentor role. I am highly motivated to contribute my life experience, maturity, and genuine passion for helping others navigate challenges and reach their potential.
I would welcome the opportunity to bring my skills and perspective to the Blues and Greens team and to continue growing as a mentor within your organisation. Thank you for your consideration, and I look forward to the possibility of discussing this role further.
Kind regards,
Howard

mentors.name: darby coleman
mentors.application_message: I am 21 living in Torquay and studying exercise and sport science in Geelong. I have a few mates already working as mentors and thought it would be a great thing to join.

mentors.name: Aidan Librio
mentors.application_message:
My name is Aidan Librio, I’m 21 years old and currently studying Business at RMIT University in Melbourne. I’m a very active and motivated person who loves sport, exercise, and staying healthy. I play football for the Shepparton Bears and have grown up around team environments, which has taught me discipline, commitment, and the importance of supporting others. I’m extremely friendly, easy to get along with, and enjoy connecting with people from all backgrounds. I have a strong interest in personal growth, mentoring, and learning from others, and I’m always keen to challenge myself and improve both personally and professionally. 
I have been referred by Xavier Lewis who is a current mentor down in torquay. 
Thanks heaps :)

mentors.name: Dustin Burns
mentors.application_message: I am a 21 year old man who has just finished University and heard about this from a friend and really wish to become a mentor to young men who need that extra support

mentors.name: jack
mentors.application_message: Love helping people especially the younger generation. Love sport. Love meeting new people My biggest passion is inspiring the youth to make decisions for themselves instead of following what everyone else is doing.

mentors.name: Declan Kenny
mentors.application_message:
Gday,
 I’m 21 years old from Ballarat currently living in Geelong while studying a double degree in sports science and business.
 Was recommended to blues and greens by a couple members the team. I love spending time with family and mates, love any kind of sport or physical activity and am super passionate about helping others grow, whether that’s personal development, in sports, or just life in general.

mentors.name: Andrew Horwood
mentors.application_message:
Just retired, ran businesses all my life, was a cris care counselor 25 years ago as well as a ips ( intensive personal support) mentor. This involved ten hours a week one on one with young males to help them in life
Lots of other volunteer work also 
3 successful children all married, independent and doing well in life

mentors.name: Oliver Moxon
mentors.application_message: I am 18 years old and I’m a full time professional triathlete living by myself on the Gold Coast training with the Australian team.My life mostly consists of training,working and spending time with my friends/family.I have a passion for being social and always making conversations.While I love to talk I also listen just as much, as I’ve learnt through sport that you have to listen to advice and encouragement from people who believe in you.Since a young age I have always loved a teamwork environment competing in multiple team sports but also a peer mentor to the year 7s at high school.I am a big believer in setting goals and achieving them I have practised this in multiple areas of life from representing my country to being able to move out of home at 18 and I know how important setting goals can be and the affect it can have on a person.I have always wanted to become a coach and this is something that I’m going to chip away at over the next few years and I believe this could be a good step in the right direction.For the past 3 years I have worked at multiple cafes where I have built a strong communication skills.
mentors.brodie_notes: old laptop problems, got a new laptop. Send a link to do again. Sending the link to his best mate.

mentors.name: Will Christie
mentors.application_message: I am a 25 year old Engineer in Geelong that is seeking fulfillment in mentoring young men to make a difference. I enjoy interacting with young people which started in my family being the eldest of 10 cousins. I play local football and always take time to check in with the under 18 players who I take a genuine interest in. I am good friends with Brodie Paul who recommended I join Blues and Greens as a mentor.

mentors.name: Ted Challenor
mentors.application_message: To whomever this concerns - My name is Ted. The moment I read the description for this role on seek, I was sold. I am 18 years old and completed high school in 2025. I graduated Ambrose Treacy College as School Captain with an ATAR of 92.2. I am commencing university this year. Throughout my captaincy my eyes were opened. I had just come back from an immersion trip to India late in 2024, and I was ready to begin a big year. That trip shaped me, and allowed me to authentically connect with young men at the all boys school I attended. I was focused on creating a community and culture of belonging through the senior theme "Stronger Together". I had the most enjoyable year of my life, and I recently returned from a solo travel trip to Vietnam which was incredible. I have been lucky enough to experience so much at such a young age and my passion truly lies in helping young people, specifically men. I currently coach at my old school, work in hospitality, and am actively searching for new opportunities. This just clicked when I read it. It was if someone had taken words out of my brain. I read the age bracket 19-25 and am aware of this - I turn 19 in July, however I am open to being involved in any way in this organisation as it truly lies with my ethos. I love my sport, gym, fitness, and understanding the world more everyday. I would bring curiosity and knowledge to a demographic I am extremely familiar with, along with my personal experiences of struggle and hardship through adolescence, and how I have progressed to become a happier individual. Hope to hear from you soon! - Ted

mentors.name: Naite Hoskin
mentors.brodie_notes: Submitted, but apparently we can’t see it? Seems good though.
mentors.norri_notes: You liked him to answer the questions well. He had the test gorilla with only the three questions, which is a shame because I would have liked to perhaps see a little more; however the way he answered the three questions on this particular test gorilla was awesome. Particularly liked his one using the Japanese word Kaizen, meaning wanting to be 1% better every day, but looks cool, speaks well, seems like a friendly, warm kind of fella that would easily engage with young blokes. Invite to group interview.

mentors.name: Ethan Williams
mentors.brodie_notes: Cant do the test gorilla on phone?

mentors.name: Matthew Glanville
mentors.norri_notes: works in school with vunrable cohort non mainstream school as a youth worker.  done research knew a lot about BG 26 yrs old Aussie nation champ in table tennis really kind face, spoke really well think he’ll be great

mentors.name: Dante Iacovone
mentors.application_message: My name is Dante Iacovone I’m 19 years old. Things I love to do is play sports, spend time with friends and family and go to the beach. These past few years I’ve gotten to understand the importance that building good connections with others can do for someone’s overall health and wellbeing. I love helping others and I think this role would be very rewarding for me and to help me develop more life skills along the way and hopefully be able to make a positive impact in others lives
mentors.norri_notes: applied in Jan by the looks - a bit monotone be interested to see what Bill thinks - didn’t give much away - only 3 questions interview which I feel doesn't allow a lot to go by

mentors.name: Riverson Robbibs
mentors.application_message:
Super super keen to get into this space! 

I love helping people & especially an opportunity to help & guide young boys/men is an opportunity I can’t pass up!

Have done a lot of growth/work on myself in last couple of years & believe I can be there for people.

Have experience as current model, also working in health insurance but degree is in biomedical science.

Always curious and looking to learn.

Looking foward to hearing from you guys :)

mentors.name: Christian Saad
mentors.application_message: Hi Harry hope you are well, my name is Christian. I'm currently a 2nd year psychology student with interests in philosophy, psychology, neuroscience and in particular mental health. I didn't always have these interests. They grew out of my struggle with severe obsessive compulsive disorder and depression. I've lived with these conditions all my life and they really rocked the way I saw the world in unhealthy ways for a long time. My psychosocial development was not in a good place for my mental wellbeing. After lots of therapy, a brutal wrestle with my inner self and 9 psychiatric hospital admissions, I feel I'm finally at a place where I'm ready to share what I learned to give hope to someone else struggling. I've very recently started my own psychology page on Instagram hoping to share another side of this struggle that often goes unmentioned and give people insights they may not otherwise hear. I've given a talk at my church community to over 100 people at the misconceptions around mental health and how to correct them. I'm looking to expand my platform and methods of communication and outreach regarding mental health. What you have done and continue to do means a lot to me and I'm so grateful for all your work. I feel I have so much to share but haven't yet found a platform to do so and I would love to join the team. Keen to hear from you and the team soon.
mentors.norri_notes: has had a lot of mental heath issues. OCD as a young fella - loved his answer as to why he chooses BG found him quite intriguing - 2nd year Psychology student

mentors.name: Bailey Frauenfelder
mentors.application_message:
I'm a 26 year old male, Based in a country Victoria, still stuck in that phase of working full time but not excatly fullfilled with the work I'm doing and looking for something a bit more rewarding. 
Then this oppurtunity popped across my desk and thought wow this could be a great oppurtunity,
I love my sports, gym and just being active, being an older brother to four younger siblings i know what it is like for someone to need you and just someone to look up to
mentors.norri_notes: a random no connection - didn’t know anything about Bg but seems to have done his research  - loved his skill to teach to tie a tie practical life skill I loved it…26 top end of age  could be really relatable to the older cohort of our boys - nose ring tats etc.   Coaches U18’s footy - would question his availablity ? I liked him a cool calm energy

mentors.name: William Worsfold
mentors.brodie_notes: like him, doing it again tonight.
mentors.norri_notes: knows some of our mentors didn't say who can solve a Rubik's cube in 2 minutes answers were short and sweet  seemed quiet young -   not sure how he would go connecting might be worth putting him to Bill he’s probably not much older than Bill

mentors.name: Hugh Dwyer
mentors.norri_notes: at Uni studyingPT and Physio - didn’t show a lot of energy - although seems like a “nice bloke” would question his availablity with a heavy uni load.. has brothers good communicator though articulates himself well  seems the real curious type which is a good trait

mentors.name: James Washington
mentors.norri_notes: Reminds me of Shane to look at - friend of Adian L works in Primary School good energy definite move to speak to Bill

mentors.name: Ofir Alkozer
mentors.norri_notes: seems like he had a bit of a rough teenage years - seemed quiet serious  hard to predict his age.. lacked energy not sure if it was nerves though…..could be good for those boys who are really isolated perhaps struggling with friends etc….he did struggle to articulate himself

mentors.name: Liam Teague
mentors.application_message: Friends with a few boys who do this as mentors and believe I can do a similar role to them. Im a hard worker own my own business and play footy. I train alot and could definitely motivate tennagers. Also never done drugs or anything so I wont encourage that either.
mentors.norri_notes: Victoria - Bendigo - uni / footy /life guarding has own window cleaning business and is really keen to learn about AI looks like Blair 🙂 good talker mates with Xavier Lewis (he said Xavier was a bit older than him - so think he must be around 18/19, worth a chat with Bill

mentors.name: Marcus Miller
mentors.referred_by: my mum saw the seek page and said this is exactly what I was looking for
mentors.norri_notes: Taught how to play a chord on a gutiar it was random but cool seemed cool - a music producer be great for the kids who love music into the arts

mentors.name: Ravnoor Singh
mentors.application_message:
Hi my name is Rav, I am someone who is very passionate about helping young people and giving them a safe space where they can be themselves. As someone who has been through the therapy route as a teen, I know the struggles of having to have a serious chat with an adult.

My hobbies include most sports, throughout my life I have played a high level of competitive cricket. I also currently work part time at a financial planning firm which is something I enjoy, additonally I go uni studying a bachelor of commerce and arts majoring in financial planning and indonesian. 

In my free time I love catching up with friends, playing sports and watching youtube.

mentors.name: Alex Hegney
mentors.application_message:
I’m driven by purpose in life, personal development, and helping teenage boys become strong, grounded, and positive men. 

I’m a self-made entrepreneur who has built a six-figure business over the past two years, and personal development is central to how I live. I read self development books for two hours a day, and apply the best of what I learn daily. 

I’ve worked with people with disabilities through Nulsen, developing empathy, patience, and leadership in real-world settings. I live a disciplined, healthy lifestyle and mentorship is a long-term calling for me, not a job. 

I’ve had a couple great mentors in my life thus far, and it’s time for me to start giving back, reach out to me via my number 0429 385 777.

mentors.name: miles dalgliesh
mentors.application_message: I am a 20 year old lad that has relocated to the big smoke (Melbourne) to tackle my academic dreams. It was in Melbourne I learnt a lot about myself I learnt that I love running gym and moving my body and connecting with others in any way possible. My eyes were also opened to the negative culture of gambling and drinking while I still participate in the occasional beer I have learnt control and that it is a drink to celebrate not to cure being bored or feeling alone. I have also been lucky enough to work with two NGO’s through uni one with a youth group which I found a really passion for educating youth and influencing them in the right directions. Cheers

mentors.name: Noah Cockerell
mentors.application_message: I recently moved to Geelong for footy and uni. One of my housemates recommended applying and thinks with my qualities I would be well suited to this form of work.

mentors.name: Lachlan
mentors.application_message: My name is Lachie, I’m currently inspiring to have a role like this outside of my current work situation, i have previously played football at the Calder Cannons with also having the privilege to captain my school first football team, currently pursing a career in the SANFL, and have been highly recommended a role like this by current mentors with you guys, would really appreciate and love the opportunity

mentors.name: Amin Naim
mentors.application_message: My name is Amin Naim, and I have a huge passion for helping younger people and guiding them. I was vice captain at the Calder Cannons and always have been regarded as a leader by my peers. People have always sought advice and encouragement from me and it’s something I’m super passionate about. I am a support worker as well so this opportunity would be a positive extension to that. I would be super grateful if I was to be taken on board.

mentors.name: Jack Henderson
mentors.application_message: I am 19 year old that loves to build relationships with others. I love surfing, football and travelling. I am going into my second year on a VFL list at the Werribee football club. I have worked with kids from the age of 5-17 years old, doing football coaching and fitness coaching. I will start my university degree this year. I am really passionate about mental health, and I would love to help be a positive influence for young kids.

mentors.name: Jett Cara
mentors.application_message: I’m a second-year university student currently studying a Bachelor of Primary and Secondary Education at ACU Melbourne with a strong passion for mentoring and supporting young people. I was recommended by Elliot Mahony’s mother for this job and I have over five years’ experience working with children and teenagers through before and after school care with The Extend Group, as well as mentoring younger umpires in my role with the Essendon District Football League. Being involved in sport as both a player and an official has helped me develop strong communication, leadership and conflict-management skills, particularly when working with teenage boys in high-pressure environments. I pride myself on being reliable, approachable and a positive role model, and I’m motivated to help young people build confidence, resilience and positive life skills.

mentors.name: Josh Gould
mentors.application_message: I’m a third year university student studying commerce and science at Monash University. I’m a very social person, and love communicating with others stemming from my debating and public speaking background in high school. In this time I was also involved in a mentorship programme, except on the receiving end for my studies and preparing for a future career and life. I am currently involved in a university committee where we work with others to organise events for other students to enjoy their time at University and make connections. I am also currently working as a bartender, serving customers and making small talk to ensure they have a pleasant experience.

mentors.name: Benjamin Kaipau
mentors.application_message:
Hey team at Blues & Greens.

I am a 29-year-old male who does support work and is finishing up a bachelor of psychology at ECU Joondalup this year. I love working with people and in mental health, and this passion had started when I volunteered for 20talk (a mental health charity) for about five years. I recently got in touch with Simon who works at Joondalup hospital, and he told me about this role and that I could be a worthy fit to be a mentor for young boys. 

I am interested in finding out more information :)

Kind regards,
Ben.

mentors.name: Aidan Cole
mentors.application_message: Hey! I’m Aidan, I’m a 20 year old male from Bacchus Marsh currently spending most of my time in Geelong. I’m a former full time competitive boxer, winning state titles and representing Victoria numerous times. I’m currently studying my cert 3/4 in fitness looking to become a personal trainer. I think being a mentor would be really beneficial for me, having a rough time growing up, I understand how easy it can be to go down some really dark paths, and I’m thankful that I had a support system that pushed me to go down the right paths, and I’m looking to do the same for the next generation of young males!

mentors.name: Joshua Chu
mentors.application_message: Referred by Ben Cantwell. I'm a 20 year old guy from Melbourne, I've been working in disability support work for the past 2 years, aiding many people to participate in daily/social activities. I enjoy all things sports, playing, watching, teaching. I love engaging with people, meeting new people and making new experiences.

mentors.name: Jett Hartman
mentors.application_message: My name is Jett, I’m 20 and live in the eastern suburbs of Melbourne in the Yarra valley. I play VFL footy for st kilda and am studying to be a Dietitian. I also volunteer for a company called ylead where I get to travel around the country and overseas teaching students and young people about leadership and help underprivileged children in countries such as Cambodia. I have a passion for the next generation and making a difference in the world. After my study I also plan to go back and do a degree in physics. I would love to hear more about what the program is about and what it requires from its mentors.

mentors.name: Ethan King
mentors.brodie_notes: SELECTED LESS THAN 2 Hrs COMMITMENT FLAG

mentors.name: Timothy Courtman
mentors.application_message: I am a 37 year old male. Qualified Youth Worker and also actor as well. Raised by a single mum. Made some mistakes and misteps in my life and looking to give back to the next generation through my own experiences. I believe its important to learn from others before you and through trial and error. Life is a journey.
